sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 237791, "Wildcat Creek", Stream, Contra Costa Co., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":237791,"gaz_name":"Wildcat Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Contra Costa"} — geometry multipoint; first point (mouth) converts to lon -122.3886, lat 37.9533 at San Pablo Bay near North Richmond, matching Wikipedia's cited mouth coordinate (37°57′12″N 122°23′19″W); this corrects the file's prior coordinate (-122.3196, 37.9134), which sat ~7.5km inland and did not match either GNIS-derived endpoint (mouth or source).

parameters.note: Wildcat Creek and all its tributaries (Contra Costa Co.) are closed to all fishing all year. Wildcat Creek supports a run of federally threatened Central California Coast steelhead, and a fish ladder at the lower creek in North Richmond is a documented passage barrier the county has been working to replace.

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to all fishing all year, on Wildcat Creek and all its tributaries.
regulations.verbatimText: (122) Wildcat Creek and tributaries (Contra Costa Co.). Closed all year to all fishing.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license. This water is closed to all fishing all year, so the license requirement is moot here, but is included for statewide-standard completeness.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
